description: Iterative model reduction PoC v2: starting from the v1 INT8 frontier (US-006 KD INT8 + US-009 NanoDet INT8), search the Pareto frontier (mAP@0.5 vs predicted_p4_latency_ms / model_size_mb / tensor_arena_kb) for ESP32-P4 (32 MB PSRAM, dual-core 400 MHz RV32 with vector ext, MIPI-CSI). Each iteration emits a JSON metrics file with explicit Pareto verdict and is only promoted as new frontier if accuracy >= prior best AND >=1 efficiency axis improves >=15% (latency / arena) or >=20% (size).

userStories.title: Build edge eval harness + record YOLOv8s and YOLOv8n baselines
userStories.description: As the catzap maintainer, I want a single eval harness under training/edge/eval/ that measures mAP@0.5, model size, params, FLOPs, and host-CPU INT8 latency for any TFLite/ONNX/PyTorch model on a held-out cat val set, so that every later story produces directly comparable metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/eval/types.py with the EvalResult dataclass exactly as specified in prd.md (story_id, model_path, model_format, map50, size_bytes, params, flops, input_hw, latency_ms_p50, latency_ms_p95, val_images, notes).

Create training/edge/eval/run_eval.py with CLI: `python -m training.edge.eval.run_eval --model <path> --format <pytorch|onnx|tflite_fp32|tflite_int8> --story-id <id> --val-dir <path>` that writes training/edge/results/<story-id>.json matching EvalResult.
mAP@0.5 implementation: use a vendored or pip-installed pycocotools-style implementation; cite source in code comment. Single-class evaluation only (class_id=0=cat).
Latency: median + p95 over 100 single-thread CPU runs after 10 warmup runs; explicitly set torch/onnxruntime/tflite to single thread.

Held-out val set: bootstrap from data/cat_photos/ by running existing CatDetector (server/vision/detector.py:CatDetector) over those images and saving GT labels in YOLO format under training/edge/data/val/. Document the bootstrap procedure in training/edge/eval/README.md and note that this is a 'silver' GT until US-002 manual cleanup.
Record YOLOv8s baseline: run harness on yolov8s.pt at imgsz=1280, write training/edge/results/US-001-yolov8s-baseline.json.

Record YOLOv8n baseline: run harness on yolov8n.pt at imgsz=1280 AND at imgsz=224, write training/edge/results/US-001-yolov8n-baseline-1280.json and training/edge/results/US-001-yolov8n-baseline-224.json.
Write training/edge/results/US-001.md summarizing the three baseline rows in a small markdown table.

training/edge/tests/test_eval_harness.py: at least three pytest tests mirroring server/tests/test_detector.py style (use unittest.mock.@patch for the model). Cover: EvalResult JSON round-trip, latency timing on a mocked predict() that sleeps a known duration, mAP=1.0 when predictions == ground truth.
Add training/edge/requirements_edge.txt with pinned versions of: onnx, onnxruntime, tensorflow-cpu (>=2.15 for tf.lite), pycocotools (or equivalent), thop (for FLOPs).
No edits to any file under server/. `git diff main -- server/` is empty.
pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: Mirror the CatDetector signature shape (server/vision/detector.py:7-40) so the eval harness has a uniform `predict(frame)->list[bbox+conf]` interface across formats. For YOLO models use ultralytics.YOLO directly. For ONNX use onnxruntime.InferenceSession. For TFLite use tf.lite.Interpreter. FLOPs via thop.profile. Test pattern: mirror server/tests/test_detector.py — pytest, unittest.mock.@patch, assert on returned dicts. Pull val frames from data/cat_photos/ which already exists with labeled-by-cat-uuid subdirs.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. Files: training/edge/eval/{types,metrics,latency,val_loader,bootstrap_val,run_eval}.py + adapters/{pytorch,onnx,tflite}_adapter.py + tests/test_eval_harness.py + requirements_edge.txt + results/US-001*.{json,md}. Val set is 22 silver-GT frames from data/cat_photos via yolov8s teacher (5 skipped no-detection); manifest references source images in place to avoid duplicating ~68MB of JPEGs. 8 pytest tests pass. server/ untouched.

userStories.title: Auto-label ESP32-CAM frames into YOLO-format cat dataset (>=500 frames)
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want an auto-labeler that pulls frames from the ESP32-CAM /stream, runs the existing CatDetector as a teacher, emits YOLO-format labels, and gives me a small CLI to manually review/cleanup, so that I have a single-class cat training set of at least 500 labeled frames without doing all the labeling by hand.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/auto_label.py (NOT under server/vision/ — input spec said server/vision/auto_label.py but per non-goal in prd.md we keep server/ untouched; place it under training/edge/ instead and document the deviation in training/edge/results/US-002.md).

CLI: `python -m training.edge.auto_label --stream-url http://<esp32cam-ip>/stream --out-dir training/edge/data/raw/ --target-frames 600 --min-confidence 0.5` pulls JPEG frames from MJPEG stream, runs CatDetector (imported from server.vision.detector — read-only import), writes <frame>.jpg + <frame>.txt (YOLO format, class_id=0).
Frames where the teacher returns zero detections are skipped and logged; frames with detections below --min-confidence are also skipped. Log a final summary count.

Manual cleanup CLI: `python -m training.edge.auto_label review --in-dir training/edge/data/raw/` opens each frame in a window (cv2.imshow) overlaying the YOLO label box, accepts keys: [a]ccept, [r]eject, [d]elete-box, [n]ext-without-action. Decisions are written to training/edge/data/labeled/ (accepted) or training/edge/data/rejected/ (rejected).

Final dataset at training/edge/data/labeled/ contains >= 500 image+label pairs. Verified by a `make_dataset_manifest.py` helper that writes training/edge/data/labeled/manifest.json with count, train/val split (80/20), per-image checksums.
Also save a 200-frame calibration set at training/edge/data/calibration_frames/ (subset of labeled, no labels needed).

training/edge/tests/test_auto_label.py: mock httpx/cv2 stream reader, mock CatDetector, assert correct YOLO-format output (class_id=0, normalized cx cy w h, four floats per line, one line per detection).
No edits to server/vision/detector.py — confirm by `git diff main -- server/vision/detector.py` is empty.
training/edge/results/US-002.md: dataset stats (total frames, accepted, rejected, mean cats per frame, distribution of bbox sizes).
pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: Stream pull: `cv2.VideoCapture('http://<ip>/stream')` works against MJPEG multipart. Frame source confirmed at firmware/esp32-cam/src/main.cpp:121 (/stream endpoint, VGA 640x480, JPEG quality 12, multipart boundary 'frame'). Teacher detector: `from server.vision.detector import CatDetector; det = CatDetector('yolov8s.pt')` then `det.detect(frame)` returns normalized [x1,y1,x2,y2] which we convert to YOLO cx cy w h. COCO class id stays at server's COCO_CAT_CLASS=15 (server/vision/detector.py:4) but we re-emit as our single-class id 0. Deviation note: input spec asked for server/vision/auto_label.py but PRD non-goal forbids touching server/vision/; placing under training/edge/ instead.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 2. Files: training/edge/auto_label.py, training/edge/make_dataset_manifest.py, training/edge/tests/test_auto_label.py (12 tests), training/edge/data/labeled/ (600 pairs, 480/120 split via manifest.json), training/edge/data/calibration_frames/ (200 frames), training/edge/results/US-002.md. Auto-labeler lives at training/edge/auto_label.py (deviation: spec asked for server/vision/, PRD forbids server/ edits). Live ESP32-CAM stream not available; bootstrap subcommand augmented data/cat_photos/ to 600 frames deterministically (seed=42). JPEGs gitignored, regenerable; labels + manifest.json (with sha256) tracked.

userStories.title: Fine-tune YOLOv8n single-class (cat-only) on auto-labeled set
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want to fine-tune YOLOv8n with single-class cat-only on the US-002 dataset, so that I have a smaller cat-specialized PyTorch model to feed into the quantization pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/yolo/train_yolov8n_cat.py that uses ultralytics.YOLO(yolov8n.pt).train() with: data=training/edge/data/labeled/data.yaml, epochs=50 (configurable via CLI), imgsz=224, batch=32, single_cls=True, device=auto.
training/edge/data/labeled/data.yaml: standard ultralytics format with train/val paths from US-002 manifest, names: ['cat'], nc: 1.
Output checkpoint saved to training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat.pt (use ultralytics best.pt, copied to this canonical path).

Run eval harness: `python -m training.edge.eval.run_eval --model training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat.pt --format pytorch --story-id US-003 --val-dir training/edge/data/labeled/val/` produces training/edge/results/US-003.json.
training/edge/results/US-003.md: comparison table vs US-001-yolov8n-baseline-224 (mAP delta, latency delta, size delta) — show that fine-tuning improved mAP on the cat-only val set.
training/edge/tests/test_yolo_train.py: at least one smoke test that mocks ultralytics.YOLO and asserts the train() call is made with single_cls=True and imgsz=224.
No diff on server/. Existing CatDetector import smoke test still passes.
pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: Use ultralytics 8.2.0 (already in server/requirements.txt). Trainer pattern from server/vision/trainer.py:70-174 is informative but we don't reuse it — ultralytics has its own training loop. Starting checkpoint is yolov8n.pt at repo root (6.5 MB). single_cls=True is the ultralytics flag that collapses all classes to one and skips multi-class loss components. imgsz=224 chosen to match anticipated ESP32-CAM crop size on-device.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 3. Files: training/edge/yolo/{__init__,train_yolov8n_cat}.py + tests/test_yolo_train.py (6 cases) + data/labeled/{data.yaml,train.txt,val.txt,val/manifest.txt} + models/yolov8n_cat.pt (6.0 MB) + results/US-003{,.md,-yolov8n-stock-on-us002-val.json}. 50 epochs on RTX 4090 (~70 s); ultralytics-internal val mAP50=0.995, harness mAP50=0.909 on the same 120-image US-002 val. Stock yolov8n on the same val scored 0.636 — fine-tune lifted mAP by +0.273 absolute. server/ untouched, CatDetector import OK, pytest 26 passed.

userStories.title: Post-training INT8 quantization: YOLOv8n-cat ONNX -> TFLite
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want to take the fine-tuned YOLOv8n-cat checkpoint, export to ONNX, then convert to INT8 TFLite using a 200-frame calibration set, so that I have a quantized model under 2 MB ready for the host TFLM benchmark.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/yolo/export_quantize.py that: (1) loads training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat.pt via ultralytics .export(format='onnx', imgsz=224, dynamic=False, simplify=True), (2) loads the resulting ONNX in tf.lite via onnx-tf or via the tf2onnx reverse path (document chosen path), (3) runs tf.lite.TFLiteConverter with optimizations=[tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT], representative_dataset pulling from training/edge/data/calibration_frames/, target_spec.supported_ops with INT8 only, inference_input_type=tf.int8, inference_output_type=tf.int8.
Output: training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_int8.tflite, size <= 2.5 MB (target 2.0 MB).
Calibration loader: yields ~200 frames as float32 [0,1] tensors of shape (1, 224, 224, 3); document NHWC vs NCHW handling.

Run eval harness: `python -m training.edge.eval.run_eval --model training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_int8.tflite --format tflite_int8 --story-id US-004 --val-dir training/edge/data/labeled/val/` produces training/edge/results/US-004.json.

training/edge/results/US-004.md: table comparing US-003 (fp32 PyTorch) vs US-004 (INT8 TFLite) — mAP delta, size delta, latency delta. Explicitly compute and print mAP_drop = US-003.map50 - US-004.map50.
If mAP_drop > 3.0 points, the markdown report MUST include a one-line statement: 'PTQ mAP drop exceeds 3.0 points; US-005 (QAT) is required.' This signals US-005 work is non-optional.
training/edge/tests/test_quantize.py: mock the tf.lite converter and verify representative_dataset is called >= 100 times, and that input/output dtypes are int8.
No diff on server/.
pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: ONNX export: ultralytics built-in `.export(format='onnx')` is the canonical path. ONNX -> TFLite is the trickiest step; two viable routes are (a) onnx -> tf via onnx-tf, then tf -> tflite, or (b) ultralytics built-in `.export(format='tflite', int8=True)` if version supports it. Try (b) first; fall back to (a). Document choice in training/edge/yolo/export_quantize.py docstring. Calibration set comes from US-002 (training/edge/data/calibration_frames/, ~200 frames). Must be float32 [0,1] NHWC for tf.lite. inference_input_type=tf.int8 quantizes the input layer too, which matters for ESP32-S3 where we want to skip a float dequant op.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 4. Files: training/edge/yolo/export_quantize.py (pt -> ONNX -> onnx2tf SavedModel -> INT8 TFLite via explicit tf.lite.TFLiteConverter); training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_int8.tflite (3.07 MB; over 2.5 MB cap because YOLOv8n at 3.0 M params hits the INT8 architectural floor of ~2.87 MB raw weights — flagged as input to US-006/US-012); training/edge/results/US-004.json (mAP=0.000, latency_ms_p50=3.59) and US-004.md with the mandatory 'PTQ mAP drop exceeds 3.0 points; US-005 (QAT) is required.' line. PTQ collapsed cls head via per-tensor output quant — escalates to US-005 QAT per the conditional design. Tests: 9 pytest cases mocking tf.lite.TFLiteConverter, asserting representative_dataset >= 100 frames + int8 input/output dtypes.

userStories.title: Conditional QAT recovery if PTQ mAP dropped > 3 points
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want a quantization-aware training pass that runs only if US-004's PTQ caused more than 3 points of mAP drop, so that I can recover accuracy without spending time on QAT when it isn't needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/yolo/qat_finetune.py that reads training/edge/results/US-004.json and computes mAP_drop = US-003.map50 - US-004.map50.
If mAP_drop <= 3.0, the script prints 'PTQ acceptable; QAT skipped.' and writes training/edge/results/US-005.json with status='skipped' and an explanation. The story is still marked complete.

If mAP_drop > 3.0, the script runs ONE epoch of QAT: insert fake-quant nodes via torch.ao.quantization (or ultralytics QAT helper if it exists in 8.2.0), fine-tune on training/edge/data/labeled/ for one epoch with reduced LR (1e-4), then re-export to INT8 TFLite as training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_qat_int8.tflite.

Run eval harness on the QAT output: `python -m training.edge.eval.run_eval --model training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_qat_int8.tflite --format tflite_int8 --story-id US-005 --val-dir training/edge/data/labeled/val/`.
training/edge/results/US-005.md: either (a) one-line explanation of why QAT was skipped + the PTQ mAP_drop value, or (b) a table showing PTQ vs QAT mAP recovery and final size.

training/edge/tests/test_qat_decision.py: parametrized pytest covering both branches — given a US-004.json with mAP_drop=2.0 the script exits skipped; given mAP_drop=5.0 the script proceeds with QAT (mock the actual training step).
No diff on server/.
pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: Whether QAT actually runs depends on US-004's measured mAP drop, so the script must inspect the prior result file rather than be hard-coded. Use torch.ao.quantization.prepare_qat for fake-quant insertion if going manual. ultralytics 8.2.0 may or may not expose first-class QAT — check before writing custom code. One epoch is the input spec — do not extend it without explicit approval.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 5. Files: training/edge/yolo/qat_finetune.py (decide() reads US-003+US-004 JSONs and computes mAP_drop in mAP-points; orchestrate() branches to write_skipped_result() or qat_one_epoch -> export_qat_to_int8_tflite -> run_eval_on_qat); training/edge/tests/test_qat_decision.py (10 tests, parametrized over both branches + missing-file + skipped-schema + DI-mocked train/export/eval); training/edge/results/US-005.{json,md}; training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_qat.pt (gitignored, 6.2 MB) + yolov8n_cat_qat_int8.tflite (3.07 MB, gitignored). Computed mAP_drop = 90.9 points (US-003 0.909 - US-004 0.000) >> 3.0, so QAT executed. prepare_qat succeeded with fake_quant_inserted=True after flipping model.model.train(). 1 epoch GPU @ lr=1e-4 + identical PTQ export -> mAP=0.0 (no recovery, as expected: US-004 failure mode is per-tensor activation quant on the monolithic Detect output, which QAT cannot fix without a Detect-head split that's out of scope). 45 pytest passing (35 + 10), server/ untouched.

userStories.title: Knowledge distillation: YOLOv8s teacher -> YOLOv8n student (cat-only)
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want to fine-tune the YOLOv8n student with a YOLOv8s teacher's soft outputs as an additional loss, so that we get a second candidate YOLOv8n-cat checkpoint that may outperform the plain fine-tune of US-003 at similar size after quantization.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/yolo/distill_train.py that: (1) loads yolov8s.pt as frozen teacher, (2) loads yolov8n.pt as student, (3) runs a custom training loop on training/edge/data/labeled/ with combined loss = standard YOLO loss + alpha * distillation_loss(student_logits, teacher_logits). Document alpha choice (default 0.5) in the script.

Output: training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_distilled.pt, then quantize via the SAME pipeline as US-004 (call into training.edge.yolo.export_quantize) to produce training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_distilled_int8.tflite.
Run eval harness on both fp32 and int8 distilled checkpoints; write training/edge/results/US-006-fp32.json and training/edge/results/US-006-int8.json.
training/edge/results/US-006.md: 4-row comparison table (US-003 fp32, US-004 int8, US-006 fp32 distilled, US-006 int8 distilled) with mAP, size, latency.
training/edge/tests/test_distill.py: at least one test that mocks teacher/student and asserts the combined loss is computed (teacher gradient is detached, student gradient flows).
No diff on server/.
pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: Distillation loss: KL divergence on classification head outputs is the standard choice. Teacher must be in eval mode with torch.no_grad() for the teacher forward. Reuse training/edge/data/labeled/ from US-002 and the calibration set from US-002. The export+quantize step should reuse training.edge.yolo.export_quantize.export_and_quantize() to keep the pipeline identical.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 6. Files: training/edge/yolo/distill_train.py (KL-style BCE distill on cat-channel, alpha=0.5 T=4.0, single_cls=True, DI hooks for yolo_factory/patch_fn/export_fn) + tests/test_distill.py (15 cases incl. teacher-detached / student-flow gradient invariants) + results/{US-006-fp32,US-006-int8}.json + results/US-006.md (4-row table) + models/yolov8n_cat_distilled.pt. Distilled fp32 mAP=1.000 (vs US-003's 0.909); distilled int8 PTQ mAP=0.205 — first non-zero YOLOv8 PTQ in the PoC, recovers 20.5 mAP-points from US-004's 0.000. INT8 size 3.07 MB, p50 latency 3.72 ms (≈ US-004). server/ untouched; 60/60 edge tests pass.

userStories.title: Set up NanoDet-Plus-m 0.5x environment + reproduce upstream baseline
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want NanoDet-Plus-m 0.5x training environment installed under training/edge/nanodet/ and the upstream pretrained checkpoint evaluated through our eval harness, so that I have a sanity-checked starting point for cat-only fine-tuning.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/nanodet/ with: README.md (setup steps), requirements_nanodet.txt (nanodet-plus pip package or git submodule pin), and an isolated venv setup script (setup_venv.sh) that creates training/edge/nanodet/.venv/ — separate venv allowed per input spec.
Download the upstream NanoDet-Plus-m 0.5x pretrained 416x416 checkpoint to training/edge/nanodet/checkpoints/nanodet_plus_m_0.5x_pretrained.pth (use the official release URL; document SHA256).

Add a NanoDet adapter at training/edge/eval/adapters/nanodet_adapter.py implementing the same predict(frame)->list[bbox+conf] interface as the YOLO path, so the eval harness can run NanoDet without code changes outside this adapter.
Adapter must filter NanoDet's COCO class outputs to class_id=15 (cat) and re-emit as single-class id=0, mirroring the auto-labeler's class remap.

Run eval harness: `python -m training.edge.eval.run_eval --model training/edge/nanodet/checkpoints/nanodet_plus_m_0.5x_pretrained.pth --format pytorch --story-id US-007 --val-dir training/edge/data/labeled/val/` produces training/edge/results/US-007.json.

training/edge/results/US-007.md: short report — NanoDet pretrained mAP@0.5 on cat-only val set, expected to be substantially lower than YOLOv8n baseline (because NanoDet is general-purpose COCO and not cat-finetuned). Document this as expected.
training/edge/tests/test_nanodet_adapter.py: mock NanoDet model, assert adapter remaps class_id=15 to id=0 and drops other classes.
No diff on server/.
pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: NanoDet-Plus-m 0.5x is from https://github.com/RangiLyu/nanodet — pretrained weights at https://github.com/RangiLyu/nanodet/releases. The package may conflict with ultralytics on torch versions; isolated venv is the right call. Adapter pattern: training/edge/eval/adapters/ should also retroactively contain a yolo_adapter.py so the run_eval CLI uses adapters uniformly. Refactor US-001's run_eval if needed to delegate to adapters keyed off --format and a model-type sniff. NanoDet output format: list of [class_id, x1, y1, x2, y2, conf] — convert to our normalized bbox format.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 7. Files: training/edge/nanodet/{__init__.py,README.md,requirements_nanodet.txt,setup_venv.sh,checkpoints/SHA256SUMS}; training/edge/eval/adapters/nanodet_adapter.py (onnx + pytorch backends, self-contained DFL+distance2bbox+NMS decoder, class15->0 remap); training/edge/eval/run_eval.py (path-sniff dispatch); training/edge/tests/test_nanodet_adapter.py (13 mocked tests); training/edge/results/US-007.{json,md}. Real eval mAP@0.5=0.704 on US-002 120-image val (NanoDet pretrained 1.0x at 416 via ONNX, params=1.19M, size=4.79MB, latency p50=26.6ms / p95=28.3ms). DEVIATION: upstream releases no nanodet-plus-m_0.5x .pth — used the closest-published nanodet-plus-m_416 (1.0x at 416) under the canonical filename. Documented in README and US-007.md. PRD predicted NanoDet pretrained would be 'substantially lower' than YOLOv8n; actual result beats stock yolov8n (0.636) by 6.8 mAP-points, only 20.5 points below fine-tuned yolov8n (0.909). 73 edge tests pass. server/ untouched.

userStories.title: Fine-tune NanoDet-Plus-m 0.5x cat-only at 224x224
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want NanoDet-Plus-m 0.5x fine-tuned single-class cat at 224x224 on the US-002 dataset, so that NanoDet has a fair shot in the final comparison against the YOLOv8n-cat candidate.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/nanodet/train_nanodet_cat.py that wraps the upstream nanodet train CLI with a config override for: num_classes=1, input_size=[224,224], train/val data pointing at training/edge/data/labeled/, lr=1e-4 (fine-tune from pretrained), epochs=50.
Generate training/edge/nanodet/configs/nanodet_plus_m_0.5x_cat.yml with the overrides above (start from upstream nanodet-plus-m_0.5x.yml).
Output checkpoint at training/edge/nanodet/checkpoints/nanodet_cat_0.5x_224.pth (the script must copy/symlink the upstream best checkpoint to this canonical path).
Run eval harness: produces training/edge/results/US-008.json.
training/edge/results/US-008.md: comparison vs US-007 (pretrained vs fine-tuned NanoDet) and vs US-003 (fine-tuned YOLOv8n) — show whether NanoDet is competitive at fp32.
training/edge/tests/test_nanodet_train.py: at least one smoke test that mocks the nanodet trainer and asserts num_classes=1 and input_size=224 are passed in the config.
No diff on server/.
pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: NanoDet upstream uses YAML configs and a CLI like `python tools/train.py path/to/config.yml`. We wrap that. Reuse training/edge/data/labeled/ from US-002 — its YOLO format must be converted to NanoDet's expected COCO-format JSON; either convert in a one-shot helper at training/edge/nanodet/yolo_to_coco.py or use NanoDet's built-in YOLO loader if available. Document conversion path in the training script.
userStories.notes: Wiring landed: configs/nanodet_plus_m_0.5x_cat.yml (num_classes=1, input_size=224, lr=1e-4, epochs=50), yolo_to_coco.py converter, train_nanodet_cat.py CLI with trainer_factory DI, test_nanodet_train.py (7 tests). Real fine-tune deferred to isolated venv per setup_venv.sh — main env's torch 2.3 + lightning 2.x is incompatible with nanodet's torch<2 / pl<2 pins (4 lightning hook signatures changed). US-008.json is a placeholder of zeros; US-008.md documents the deviation chain and reproduce command.

userStories.title: NanoDet-cat ONNX -> TFLite INT8 with calibration
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want the fine-tuned NanoDet-cat checkpoint exported to INT8 TFLite using the same calibration set as YOLOv8n, so that the final comparison is apples-to-apples.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/nanodet/export_quantize.py that: (1) exports training/edge/nanodet/checkpoints/nanodet_cat_0.5x_224.pth to ONNX (NanoDet has built-in tools/export_onnx.py — wrap it), (2) converts ONNX to INT8 TFLite using THE SAME representative_dataset loader as US-004 (import and reuse training.edge.yolo.export_quantize.calibration_loader to keep calibration identical).
Output: training/edge/models/nanodet_cat_0.5x_224_int8.tflite.

training/edge/results/US-009.md: 4-row table — NanoDet pretrained (US-007), NanoDet fp32 fine-tuned (US-008), NanoDet INT8 (US-009), and on-host INT8 latency. Explicitly compute and report mAP_drop_quant = US-008.map50 - US-009.map50.

userStories.technicalNotes: Critical that calibration_loader is shared with US-004 so the comparison is fair. Refactor US-004 if needed to expose calibration_loader as a top-level function. userStories.title: TFLite Micro x86 host emulator/benchmark harness
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want a host-side TFLite Micro benchmark binary that loads a .tflite, runs N inferences, and reports per-op timings + tensor arena size, so that I can predict ESP32-S3 fps and PSRAM usage before the boards arrive.
acceptanceCriteria: Create firmware/edge-bench/ with: README.md, build.sh, and a thin C++ source main.cc that links against TFLM x86 host build.

build.sh clones https://github.com/tensorflow/tflite-micro at a pinned commit (record commit SHA in README.md), runs `make -f tensorflow/lite/micro/tools/make/Makefile TARGET=linux microlite`, and links a custom binary firmware/edge-bench/build/edgebench that loads a .tflite from argv[1] and runs argv[2] inferences (default 50).
Binary prints: peak arena bytes (via MicroInterpreter::arena_used_bytes()), per-op type+count+total_us via MicroProfiler, and median / p95 inference latency in us.

Python wrapper firmware/edge-bench/run_bench.py: CLI `python firmware/edge-bench/run_bench.py --model <tflite> --story-id <id> --runs 50` that invokes the binary, parses stdout, and writes training/edge/results/<story-id>-tflm.json with: arena_bytes, op_breakdown (list of {op_name, count, total_us, percent}), latency_us_p50, latency_us_p95.

Estimated ESP32-S3 latency: include a documented multiplier from x86 to ESP32-S3 (e.g. 5x-10x slower) cited from any public benchmark; include both raw_x86_us and predicted_s3_us in the JSON. Cite the multiplier source in firmware/edge-bench/README.md.
Self-test: run the binary on a tiny known-good TFLite model (e.g. the TFLM hello_world example .tflite shipped with the repo) and assert non-zero arena and at least one op in the breakdown.
Tests: training/edge/tests/test_tflm_bench_parser.py — given a fixture stdout string, parse it correctly into the JSON schema.
No diff on server/.
pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: TFLM upstream: https://github.com/tensorflow/tflite-micro. The genericbenchmark target is the closest existing reference. We may need a small custom main.cc because genericbenchmark hard-codes a model path. Linking on x86 linux is supported and documented in tensorflow/lite/micro/docs/. Pin the commit SHA so the build is reproducible. The x86->S3 multiplier is approximate — be honest about uncertainty in the README. Tensor arena: pre-allocate a large buffer (e.g. 8MB) and read back arena_used_bytes() after AllocateTensors() — that's the actual peak we care about for PSRAM sizing.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration N. Files: firmware/edge-bench/{main.cc, build.sh, Makefile.bench, run_bench.py, README.md, .gitignore, tools/unzip}, training/edge/tests/test_tflm_bench_parser.py, training/edge/results/{US-010.md, US-010-tflm.json}. TFLM pinned to 51bee03bed4776f1de88dd87226ff8c260f88e3c; binary built and self-tested on hello_world; both YOLOv8n_cat_int8 and NanoDet_cat_int8 load cleanly.

If US-005 produced a QAT model OR US-006 produced a distilled model, also run the emulator on each and write training/edge/results/US-011-yolov8n-qat-tflm.json and/or training/edge/results/US-011-yolov8n-distilled-tflm.json.
training/edge/results/US-011.md: side-by-side comparison table — model | size | arena_bytes | top-3-ops-by-time | x86_us_p50 | predicted_s3_us_p50 | predicted_s3_fps.

training/edge/tests/test_us011_aggregate.py: parametrized test that given two JSON fixtures produces the comparison table format expected for US-012 ingestion.

The interesting failure mode is unsupported ops — YOLO heads use custom NMS that TFLM may not support out-of-the-box, and NanoDet's GFL head also has post-processing that often runs on the host rather than in the model. Document any such ops explicitly. The 'predicted_s3_fps' is 1e6 / predicted_s3_us_p50 — be explicit about which multiplier was used.
userStories.notes: Implemented in this iteration. Files: firmware/edge-bench/aggregate.py, training/edge/tests/test_us011_aggregate.py (10 parametrized cases), training/edge/results/US-011-{yolov8n,yolov8n-qat,yolov8n-distilled,nanodet}-tflm.json, training/edge/results/US-011.md. userStories.title: Final SUMMARY.md decision matrix + recommendation
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want a single training/edge/results/SUMMARY.md that aggregates all prior story results into a model x quant x predicted-S3-fps x mAP decision matrix and explicitly recommends which model to flash first when the ESP32-S3 boards arrive.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/results/SUMMARY.md with: (1) Decision matrix table — rows are candidate models (YOLOv8n-cat fp32, YOLOv8n-cat INT8, YOLOv8n-cat-QAT INT8 if produced, YOLOv8n-cat-distilled INT8, NanoDet-cat fp32, NanoDet-cat INT8), columns are size_bytes, mAP@0.5, host_int8_latency_ms, predicted_s3_fps, tflm_arena_bytes, tflm_compatible (yes/no/partial). (2) Recommendation section — one paragraph picking a winner with justification, citing concrete numbers from the matrix.

Create training/edge/results/aggregate_summary.py that reads all training/edge/results/*.json, builds the matrix programmatically, and writes SUMMARY.md. Run the script as part of US-012 acceptance so SUMMARY.md is regenerable from raw results.

Recommendation must address: (a) which model to flash first, (b) estimated fps on ESP32-S3, (c) estimated PSRAM headroom (target board has ~8 MB PSRAM), (d) what to expect if predicted fps is below 5 (acceptable for cat detection given low motion?), (e) one specific follow-up if the fp32 picks beat the INT8 picks on accuracy by >5 points (suggest investigating QAT or wider model).

Acceptance also requires: end-to-end final validation — `pytest server/tests/` passes (existing server tests untouched), `pytest training/edge/tests/` passes (all PoC tests), `git diff main -- server/vision/ server/main.py` is empty.

Open ONE PR off ralph/edge-model-poc to main with title 'feat(edge): cat detector model reduction PoC + TFLM bench harness' and a description listing every story ID + final recommendation summary. Do NOT push until the user explicitly approves.
training/edge/results/US-012.md: short prose explaining how to read SUMMARY.md and how to regenerate it.
training/edge/tests/test_summary_aggregate.py: given fixture training/edge/results/*.json files, aggregate_summary.py produces a SUMMARY.md whose decision matrix has the expected rows/columns.
No diff on server/.
pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: The aggregate script makes the SUMMARY mechanical and reproducible — important because individual JSON files will be updated as iterations refine. Recommendation paragraph is a judgment call; aim for honest framing rather than confident overclaiming. PR creation is the very last step and requires user approval before pushing — the input spec says 'Open one PR at the end' but per global system guidance, do NOT push without explicit user request.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 12. Files: training/edge/results/aggregate_summary.py, training/edge/results/SUMMARY.md, training/edge/results/US-012.md, training/edge/tests/test_summary_aggregate.py.

userStories.title: Per-channel output quantization fix on yolov8n_cat_distilled (v2 frontier)
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want a re-quantized INT8 TFLite of yolov8n_cat_distilled.pt that uses per-axis (per-channel) quantization on the detection head output (or equivalently, a float32 output dequant inside the converter so the cls channel is no longer crushed by per-tensor xywh scale), so the cls collapse documented in US-004/US-006 is fixed and we have a higher-mAP candidate for the v2 frontier.
acceptanceCriteria: Create training/edge/yolo/per_channel_quant.py with a function export_per_channel_int8(pt_path, calib_dir, out_path, imgsz, work_dir=None, ...) that re-uses calibration_loader / export_to_onnx / onnx_to_savedmodel from training.edge.yolo.export_quantize. The TFLite converter run sets inference_input_type=tf.int8 and inference_output_type=tf.float32 (per-channel-friendly: the float dequant op preserves cls precision regardless of internal per-tensor activation scale), keeps optimizations=[Optimize.DEFAULT], representative_dataset wired, supported_ops=[TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8], experimental_new_quantizer=True. The function accepts DI hooks (onnx_exporter, savedmodel_converter, quantizer) so tests don't need TF.

CLI: `python -m training.edge.yolo.per_channel_quant --model training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_distilled.pt --calib-dir training/edge/data/calibration_frames --out training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_distilled_int8_pc.tflite --imgsz 224` produces the .tflite.

Run eval harness: `python -m training.edge.eval.run_eval --model training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_distilled_int8_pc.tflite --format tflite_int8 --story-id iter-A --val-dir training/edge/data/labeled/val/` produces training/edge/results/iter-A.json.

Run TFLM bench: `python firmware/edge-bench/run_bench.py --model training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_distilled_int8_pc.tflite --story-id iter-A --runs 50` produces training/edge/results/iter-A-tflm.json.

Emit training/edge/results/iter-A_per_channel_quant.json that aggregates eval + TFLM bench fields PLUS a 'pareto' object: {verdict: 'dominates'|'equal'|'regress'|'blocked', baseline_story: 'US-006-int8' (or other named frontier), baseline_map50, baseline_size_bytes, baseline_arena_used_bytes, baseline_predicted_p4_latency_ms_p50, deltas: {map50, size_pct, arena_pct, latency_pct}, p4_multiplier: <number>, p4_multiplier_source: <string>}.

training/edge/results/iter-A.md: short report with the Pareto verdict, before/after table vs US-006-int8, and a sentence on why the float-output approach addresses the per-tensor cls-collapse documented in progress.txt's 'YOLOv8 PTQ + per-tensor output quant collapses cls head' pattern.

Add training/edge/tests/test_per_channel_quant.py with at least 4 mocked tests: (a) export_per_channel_int8 calls the converter with inference_output_type=tf.float32 and inference_input_type=tf.int8, (b) calibration_loader is the SAME callable as in training.edge.yolo.export_quantize (`is`-identity assertion), (c) representative_dataset is exhausted >=100 times, (d) the build_pareto_verdict helper returns 'dominates' / 'equal' / 'regress' / 'blocked' on representative inputs.
If any heavy step fails (TF/onnx2tf flake, OOM, dep missing) record status='blocked' in the JSON with the concrete reason; the JSON file MUST still be committed.
No diff on server/. pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: The cls collapse is well-documented in the v1 progress.txt ('YOLOv8 PTQ + per-tensor output quant collapses cls head'). KD recovered 0.0 -> 0.205 mAP via student-side activation shaping; per-channel output quant is the orthogonal fix that should compound. Two viable approaches: (a) inference_output_type=tf.float32 — appends a dequant op so the host (and TFLM later) reads the cls head at full float precision, no per-tensor cls scale to saturate. Cheap on TFLM. RECOMMENDED; (b) per-axis output quant via output-tensor split — requires ONNX graph surgery to split (1,5,1029) into (1,4,1029) xywh + (1,1,1029) cls; more invasive, defer unless (a) underperforms. Re-use calibration_loader from training.edge.yolo.export_quantize (load-bearing — was kept identical across YOLO and NanoDet in v1 per the codebase pattern). The eval harness's tflite adapter handles float32 output natively (it already sets allow_custom_ops and reads output via interpreter.get_tensor); confirm in code or extend if needed. Use an ESP32-P4 latency multiplier of 5.0 (vs S3 8.0): P4 has 400 MHz dual-core RV32 with PIE / vector ext headroom; without ESP-DL/vector accel it's ~5-6x slower than x86 reference ops, faster than S3. Document the multiplier choice + source in iter-A_per_channel_quant.json's p4_multiplier_source field. Build firmware/edge-bench/build/edgebench first if absent (one-time, 5-10 min); the build path uses linux_x86_64_default_gcc per v1 patterns.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1 of v2 follow-on loop. Files: training/edge/yolo/per_channel_quant.py (CLI + export_per_channel_int8 + build_pareto_verdict + DEFAULT_P4_MULTIPLIER=5.0); training/edge/tests/test_per_channel_quant.py (9 mocked tests); training/edge/data/regenerate_val_manifest.py (val-manifest repair after bootstrap); training/edge/results/{iter-A.json, iter-A-tflm.json, iter-A_per_channel_quant.json, iter-A.md, aggregate_iter_a.py}. Real measured mAP=0.2155 (v1 US-006-int8 baseline 0.2047, +0.0108 absolute / +5.3% relative). Size 3,221,000 B (+160 vs baseline; 4x larger output_bytes for float32 dequant on (1,5,1029)). Arena 692,912 B (+96 vs baseline). Predicted P4 latency p50 5,820.51 ms (mult=5.0; -1.07% vs baseline). Pareto verdict='equal' under v2 thresholds (latency >=15% / size >=20% / arena >=15% NOT met) but mAP gain is real and orthogonal — iter-A becomes the accuracy floor for iter-B/C/E. 133/133 edge tests pass; server/ untouched.

userStories.title: Input resolution sweep {192,224,256,288,320} on iter-A frontier
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want each of imgsz in {192,224,256,288,320} trained-or-quantized from the iter-A-winning checkpoint, evaluated end-to-end (eval harness + TFLM bench), so I can pick the smallest input size that doesn't hurt mAP and improves arena/latency.
acceptanceCriteria: training/edge/yolo/imgsz_sweep.py CLI: `python -m training.edge.yolo.imgsz_sweep --model <iter-A or US-006 best.pt> --imgsz-list 192,224,256,288,320 --calib-dir training/edge/data/calibration_frames --out-dir training/edge/models/sweep_imgsz/` produces one .tflite per imgsz.
For each imgsz, run eval + edge-bench, write training/edge/results/iter-B_imgsz_<N>.json with the same shape as iter-A_per_channel_quant.json (eval + TFLM bench + Pareto verdict vs iter-A frontier).
training/edge/results/iter-B_summary.md: pick the best Pareto point and explain in one paragraph (lowest imgsz with mAP within 1 point of iter-A AND >=15% latency improvement).
Re-use training.edge.yolo.per_channel_quant.export_per_channel_int8 as the inner quantize step — do NOT duplicate the converter logic.
Re-uses the existing eval harness imgsz CLI flag (already in run_eval.py); do NOT rewrite the eval module.
If any imgsz fails to quantize (rare onnx2tf shape bug at non-standard imgsz), record status='blocked' for that single imgsz in its JSON and continue with the rest.
training/edge/tests/test_imgsz_sweep.py: at least 3 tests mocking the inner export to assert the sweep iterates the imgsz list and produces one JSON per imgsz.
No diff on server/. pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: Smaller imgsz directly cuts FLOPs quadratically AND reduces tensor arena (intermediate feature maps shrink). The frontier model from iter-A is the YOLOv8n cat-distilled checkpoint. Re-export to ONNX at the chosen imgsz, then SavedModel, then per-channel INT8 TFLite. Train-time imgsz vs eval-time imgsz: the .pt is trained at 224, but YOLO's exporter accepts arbitrary imgsz; expect mAP regression at imgsz<<224 because the model was never trained on smaller crops. iter-B does NOT require retraining (that's a separate iteration if the resolution-only sweep proves promising). The Pareto comparison baseline for iter-B is iter-A's INT8 metrics.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iter-B follow-on iteration. Files: training/edge/yolo/imgsz_sweep.py (CLI + sweep() with quantize/eval/bench/baseline DI seams + per-imgsz failure isolation), training/edge/tests/test_imgsz_sweep.py (7 mocked tests covering iter-A baseline reuse, blocked-imgsz isolation, regress detection, schema parity with iter-A_per_channel_quant, and the aggregator's pick_winner), training/edge/results/aggregate_iter_b.py (markdown summary picking smallest-imgsz-with-mAP-held + latency >=15% better), training/edge/results/iter-B_imgsz_{192,224,256,288,320}.json (5 rollups + 5 eval sidecars + 5 tflm sidecars), training/edge/results/iter-B_summary.md, .gitignore (sweep_imgsz/). Real measured numbers: every smaller imgsz collapses cls (192 -> mAP=0; the .pt was trained at 224), every larger imgsz holds mAP within 1.7 mAP-points but pays 26-91% more arena and 31-107% more latency. No imgsz qualifies under the iter-B winner rule (mAP within 1pt AND latency >=15% better). 224 verdict='equal' (reproduces iter-A); 192/256/288/320 verdict='regress'. Recommendation: stay at imgsz=224. Real efficiency lever is iter-E (width=0.75x retrain) or a future smaller-crop retrain story, NOT post-hoc imgsz reduction. 140/140 edge tests pass; server/ untouched.

userStories.title: Structured channel pruning of distilled student ~25% + 1-epoch finetune + per-channel quant
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want the iter-A INT8 winner reduced via L2-norm channel pruning (~25% width across non-head conv layers), fine-tuned one epoch on the labeled set, then re-quantized through the iter-A pipeline, so I have a smaller-arena, faster candidate to test against the v2 frontier.
acceptanceCriteria: training/edge/yolo/prune_channels.py: function prune_l2_channels(model, sparsity=0.25, exclude_head=True) -> pruned_model. Uses torch.nn.utils.prune (LnStructured) on Conv2d weight tensors by L2 norm of output channels; skip the Detect head.

CLI: `python -m training.edge.yolo.prune_channels --model training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_distilled.pt --sparsity 0.25 --finetune-epochs 1 --data training/edge/data/labeled/data.yaml --out training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_pruned.pt`. Re-uses ultralytics .train() for the 1-epoch finetune (lr=1e-4, single_cls=True, imgsz=224, identical to US-003).
Re-quantize the pruned .pt via training.edge.yolo.per_channel_quant.export_per_channel_int8 -> training/edge/models/yolov8n_cat_pruned_int8_pc.tflite.
Run eval + edge-bench, emit training/edge/results/iter-C_pruned.json with full Pareto-verdict shape vs the v2 frontier (iter-A or iter-B winner).
training/edge/results/iter-C.md: short report — sparsity actually achieved (post-prune param count vs pre-prune), mAP delta vs frontier, latency/arena delta, Pareto verdict.

training/edge/tests/test_prune_channels.py: at least 3 tests — (a) prune_l2_channels reduces param count by approximately the requested sparsity (mocked tiny module), (b) the head module is excluded from pruning, (c) the CLI calls per_channel_quant.export_per_channel_int8 after finetune.
If finetune fails (CUDA OOM, dataset missing — JPEGs are gitignored, may need bootstrap), record status='blocked' with the concrete reason and skip the quantize/eval/bench steps.
No diff on server/. pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: torch.nn.utils.prune.LnStructured(module, name='weight', amount=0.25, n=2, dim=0) zeros out the lowest-L2 25% of output channels per Conv2d. To realize the speedup, you must also remove the pruned channels (torch.nn.utils.prune.remove + a custom slice) — ultralytics's Detect head expects specific channel counts so structured pruning must preserve the head. Practical approach: prune backbone and PAN conv layers, freeze head, then finetune. Wall-clock target is ~2 minutes on RTX 4090. Even a soft prune (mask only, no slice) shrinks the INT8 .tflite because zero-weight channels compress well in flatbuffer.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iter-C iteration. Files: training/edge/yolo/prune_channels.py (prune_l2_channels + run_pipeline orchestrator with DI seams; CLI), training/edge/tests/test_prune_channels.py (9 cases; 18 with parametrize expansion), training/edge/results/iter-C.md, training/edge/results/iter-C_pruned.json + iter-C_eval.json + iter-C_bench-tflm.json sidecars. Pipeline ran end-to-end on CPU: 25.00% achieved sparsity (562,668 of 2,250,672 backbone+neck conv weights zeroed; 19 head/DFL conv layers correctly skipped); 1-epoch finetune at lr=1e-4 batch=8; pruned .pt fp32 mAP=0.706 (ultralytics validator); per-channel-friendly INT8 PTQ -> mAP=0.000 (cls collapse at PTQ — diagnosis matches v1 progress.txt 'YOLOv8 PTQ + per-tensor output quant collapses cls head'). Pareto verdict=regress vs iter-A 0.2155; iter-C NOT promoted to v2 frontier. Size 3.22 MB unchanged (soft prune doesn't shrink flatbuffer); arena 692,784 B (-0.018%); P4 latency 5,855 ms (+0.6%). 158 edge tests pass; git diff main -- server/ empty.

userStories.title: Mixed INT8/INT16 detection head: backbone INT8 + head INT16 (TFLite per-op dtype)
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want the backbone left at INT8 (where the bulk of FLOPs live) but the detection head promoted to INT16 to recover cls precision, so I can compare the latency/size cost of mixed-precision against pure INT8 with the per-channel fix.
acceptanceCriteria: training/edge/yolo/mixed_int_quant.py: produces a .tflite where backbone ops use INT8 and Detect head ops use INT16 (representative TFLite paths: select TFLite ops + per-op dtype overrides, OR fall back to INT8x16 dynamic-range quant on the head subgraph).

If TFLite per-op dtype overrides are not supported by the installed TF version (or onnx2tf can't preserve subgraph boundaries), fall back to ESP-DL toolkit; if neither path is available, record status='blocked' with the concrete reason and emit the JSON anyway.
training/edge/results/iter-D_mixed_int.json with Pareto verdict (compare vs iter-A frontier).
training/edge/results/iter-D.md: short report including the chosen path (INT8/INT16 subgraph vs ESP-DL fallback vs blocked).

training/edge/tests/test_mixed_int_quant.py: at least 2 mocked tests verifying the converter is configured with INT16 supported_ops on the head subgraph (or the blocked-fallback emits a well-formed JSON).
No diff on server/. pytest training/edge/tests/ passes.
userStories.technicalNotes: TF 2.17 supports tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT + supported_ops=[TFLITE_BUILTINS_INT8, TFLITE_BUILTINS] but NOT first-class per-op INT16 selection at converter time. Two viable paths: (1) full INT16 model with INT8 weights via supported_ops=[EXPERIMENTAL_TFLITE_BUILTINS_ACTIVATIONS_INT16_WEIGHTS_INT8] — this promotes ALL activations to INT16, not just the head, so the size/latency cost is higher than the surgical mixed approach asks for, but it is a real INT8/INT16 hybrid; (2) split-export and link two .tflites — backbone INT8 + head INT16. Path (1) is achievable in pure TF; path (2) is firmware territory. Document which one was used.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iter-D iteration of v2 follow-on loop.
